entry.title: MuleSoft vs SAP Integration Suite: Which One Fits Your Enterprise?
entry.content:
STRATEGY PAPER - INTEGRATION PLATFORM ASSESSMENT

SAP Integration Suite Vs MuleSoft Anypoint Platform

Choosing an integration platform is one of the most consequential infrastructure decisions an enterprise can make. Both SAP Integration Suite and MuleSoft Anypoint Platform are mature, enterprise-grade solutions. This paper offers an objective, fact-based view of where SAP Integration Suite delivers a distinct strategic advantage for organisations whose landscape is built on SAP.

 

1. Setting the Context

While MuleSoft Anypoint Platform has long maintained a strong presence in the enterprise integration space, SAP Integration Suite, a core component of the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) has gained significant traction among organizations pursuing cloud-first and SAP-centric strategies.

Many enterprises are reassessing their reliance on MuleSoft as part of broader integration modernization initiatives. Factors such as rising total cost of ownership, challenges in optimizing integrations within SAP-heavy landscapes, and increasing demand for faster, scalable innovation are contributing to this shift.

As a result, SAP Integration Suite is increasingly being considered as a strategic alternative for organizations seeking tighter SAP alignment, improved cost efficiency, and enhanced agility in their integration landscape

The key question is not which platform is 'better', it is which platform is the right fit for your organisation's ecosystem, skill base, and strategic direction. For businesses running SAP S/4HANA, SAP SuccessFactors, SAP Ariba, or other SAP cloud solutions, the answer increasingly points to SAP Integration Suite.

 

2. Head-to-Head: Feature Comparison

The table below summarises verified, up-to-date comparisons across key integration criteria. 

 

Criteria

MuleSoft Anypoint Platform

SAP Integration Suite

Development effort

Higher complexity - Anypoint Studio (Eclipse-based IDE), Java/DataWeave expertise required. Steeper onboarding for new teams.

Lower effort - Browser-based graphical iFlow design. Pre-built SAP content packages accessible to developers and consultants. Low code, no code platform.

Message mapping

DataWeave used for mapping is proprietary and complex.

Graphical drag-and-drop mapping editor with built-in functions. Groovy/XSLT available for advanced cases. Easier to build and maintain.

Libraries

Need to import various Java libraries to develop integrations.

Most of the libraries are handled at the product level.

Event-driven architecture

No native built in event broker. External tools (RabbitMQ, Kafka, ActiveMQ) required, adding infrastructure cost and operational overhead.

SAP Event Mesh and Advanced Event Mesh is a fully managed native capability on BTP. Supports pub/sub, event streaming, multi-cloud distribution out of the box.

Transport & deployment

Changes to one flow require full application redeployment, less flexibility, risk involved and longer release cycles.

Artifact-level transport via Cloud Transport Management Service. Move individual iFlows independently across landscapes. Faster, safer releases.

SAP ecosystem connectivity

Third-party connectors or custom BAPI/RFC adapters needed for SAP systems. Additional build and maintenance effort.

Native connectors and thousands of pre-built packages on SAP Business Accelerator Hub. Zero custom work for core SAP integrations.

Custom adapter development

500+ connectors on Anypoint Exchange. SDK makes building custom connectors for non-SAP systems straightforward.

Strength in SAP-centric environment. Non-SAP custom adapters require deeper technical expertise.

Cost model

Premium vCore-based licensing. Costs can escalate unpredictably with integration volume growth.

BTP consumption-based model. Organisations with existing BTP entitlements get significant IS capacity included.

Monitoring & operations

Anypoint Monitoring provides dashboards and alerts. Comprehensive observability often requires custom configuration.

Simple Message monitoring, Alert Notification Service, Cloud ALM - all standard provided by out of the box by SAP. No additional tooling required.

Retry / error handling

Custom coding required (Until Successful scope or custom error flows). Risk of inconsistency across flows.

Built-in JMS-based retry with dead-letter queue handling. Persistent messaging, configurable without custom code.

Downtime handling

While MuleSoft CloudHub 2.0 offers zero-downtime via Rolling Updates and Blue-Green deployment, it requires specific replica configurations and vCore infrastructure overhead.

SAP Integration Suite provides seamless artifact deployment natively without additional infrastructure management.

Deployment Speed

MuleSoft deployments typically range from 3–10 minutes due to runtime initialization.

SAP Integration Suite typically completes in 10–45 seconds by updating the artifact in memory.

Salesforce Pub/Sub API(gRPC over HTTP/2)

While MuleSoft supports gRPC Connector with underlying high-performance API technology it often requires granular configuration, custom code or flow design to implement the same pub/sub streaming logic.

SAP Integration Suite provides a prebuilt Pub/Sub adapter that abstracts gRPC complexity, enabling configuration-driven consumption of Salesforce events without requiring protocol-level implementation.

 

MuleSoft offers flexibility at the cost of increased development complexity.

SAP Integration Suite reduces implementation effort and accelerates time-to-value

 

3. SAP Integration Suite — Key Strategic Differentiators

For organisations running SAP at their core, the following capabilities represent genuinely differentiated value that MuleSoft cannot replicate without additional investment.

 

BTP consumption pricing

Pay for what you use. Existing BTP entitlements often include significant Integration Suite capacity.

Advanced Event Mesh

Built-in event broker on BTP. No external Kafka/RabbitMQ needed. Real-time event streaming natively.

Artifact-level transport

UI driven, native transport mechanism to move individual iFlows across Dev → QA → Prod. No full application redeployment required.

 

Built-in monitoring

Message monitoring, Alert Notification Service, and Cloud ALM standard out of the box.

Business Accelerator Hub

Thousands of pre-built integration packages from SAP and partners. Reduces build effort significantly.

 

4. When Should Organisations Consider SAP Integration Suite?

Migration decisions should never be taken lightly. The following signals suggest a closer evaluation of SAP Integration Suite is warranted:

  • SAP-first digital core: Your organisation has standardised on S/4HANA and SAP cloud solutions. The majority of integration flows touch SAP systems.
  • MuleSoft licensing costs: Integration volumes are scaling and MuleSoft vCore costs are growing faster than the business value they deliver.
  • Legacy integration retirement: Moving off SAP PI/PO or MuleSoft - SAP Integration Suite is the natural destination for organisations consolidating on BTP.
  • Event-driven ambitions without broker overhead: If real-time event-driven patterns are on the roadmap, Event Mesh removes the need for external broker infrastructure.
  • Low integration cost: SAP Integration Suite is a low code platform requiring less development effort overall.

7. SAP Integration Suite & Salesforce — A Closer Look

Salesforce is the world's leading CRM platform. SAP is the world's leading ERP platform. Making them work together seamlessly is one of the most common, and most strategically important integration challenges enterprises face. This section gives an honest, balanced view of how SAP Integration Suite handles Salesforce integration.

An important nuance: MuleSoft is owned by Salesforce, giving it a structural first-party advantage in the Salesforce ecosystem - early API access, deep product alignment, and a large connector community. SAP Integration Suite wins on a different dimension listed above and primarily on SAP side of integration, where it has unmatched native depth.

SAP Integration Suite "Salesforce + SAP S4/HANA integration"

use cases

Use case

What it does

SAP IS advantage

Quote-to-cash

Salesforce opportunity or quote triggers SAP S4/HANA sales order creation automatically

Pre-built iFlow on SAP API Business Accelerator Hub. Native S/4HANA OData APIs used directly - no custom mapping layer needed

Customer master sync

Bidirectional sync of customer/account records between Salesforce and SAP

Handles complex SAP customer master data model models. Pre-built content manages mapping and prevents duplicate creation.

Order status updates

SAP fulfilment and delivery status pushed back to Salesforce in near-real-time

Event-driven via SAP Advanced Event Mesh. Sales reps see live order status without leaving CRM

Invoice and payment sync

SAP-generated invoices and payment status reflected in Salesforce.

Directly surfaces SAP FI/CO(Financial) data to Salesforce, ensuring accurate revenue tracking.

Real-time credit checks

Sales reps query SAP credit exposure directly from within Salesforce before quoting

On-demand SAP data pull via OData API, orchestrated by IS iFlow. Result returned to Salesforce in seconds

Event-driven order events

Salesforce Pub/Sub events consumed by SAP IS to trigger downstream SAP processes

Native Salesforce Pub/Sub adapter - real-time, no polling, no external broker required

Conclusion

For SAP-centric enterprises, SAP Integration Suite is not simply 'good enough' - it is increasingly the superior strategic choice. Key reasons (including for Salesforce integration):

•  Lower total cost of ownership when BTP entitlements are already in place.

•  Native connectivity eliminates custom adapter work for SAP applications.

•  Built-in Event Mesh removes the need for external broker infrastructure.

•  Artifact - level transport enables safer, faster release cycles.

•  Pre-packaged integration content from the Business Accelerator Hub accelerates delivery.

•  Better developer productivity due to out of box functionality by the platform.

•  Browser-based tooling integrated within the SAP ecosystem, enabling centralized development and management.

“This is an independent analysis based on project-based experience and publicly available information”

entry.title: New Partner Content on SAP Business Accelerator Hub (Q1 ’26)
entry.content:
In continuation of our previous blog, we are excited to share the latest updates and continue delivering valuable insights to our community.

In this edition of our blog series, we highlight the newest content packages (particularly APIs and Integrations) released on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub by our partners in Q1 2026, along with high-level details.

For complete details on each partner's content, see the partners listing on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub Home page.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Avalara

Integration packages published:

SAP S/4HANA Integration with Avalara Avatax Sales and Use Tax

Sales and use tax rates are varied and complex across counties, states, and countries. And because the burden of remaining compliant is on the business, there’s a big financial risk to getting it wrong. Even when manual tax processes are manageable, keeping up with the changes can be impossible — there’s always the possibility of legislative regulations, new tax rules, and shifts in product taxability. Rather than spending time ensuring your rates, product taxability, and collection requirements haven’t changed, let our cloud-based SaaS system evaluate each transaction based on the latest laws and rules, without additional effort from your IT or finance teams.

SAP S/4HANA Integration with Avalara Solution Suite

Processes such as routing, authentication and logging that are reused across different integrations have been brought together in a single package.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Incture Technologies Private Limited

Integration packages published:

Microsoft Power BI Integration with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

The integration of Microsoft Power BI with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition provides a robust and scalable architecture for advanced analytics and business intelligence. This setup leverages SAP Cloud Integration as a secure and flexible middleware to extract, transform, and deliver SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition data to Microsoft Power BI, enabling real-time and insightful data visualization.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition with Shopify

Integration between SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition with Shopify connects Shopify’s e-commerce storefront with SAP’s ERP core. Shopify manages online sales and customer experience, while SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition handles order processing, inventory, pricing, fulfillment, and financials. Data is synchronized in near real time using SAP Cloud Integration.

SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll To Banamex ACH

The integration between SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll (ECP) and Banamex ACH enables secure and automated transmission of payroll data from SAP’s cloud-based payroll system to the bank for salary disbursement. The solution ensures accurate, compliant, and timely processing of employee payments.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Figaf ApS

Integration package published:

Figaf Integration with ServiceNow

The "Figaf Integration with ServiceNow" package provides a template for integrating Figaf and SNow. It includes highly configurable iFlows processing Figaf webhooks for ticket/transport events to SNow actions, update Figaf based on SNow changes, and a utility for searching tasks.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

QuantityWare GmbH

Integration package published:

QuantityWare Integration with Third Party

The integration between QuantityWare Bulk Calculations Solution (BCS) and non-SAP environments through SAP BTP, provides customers the ability to access BCS from any application or external interface. This expands QuantityWare BCS usage beyond the SAP environment, allowing greater flexibility and integration potential.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SPS Commerce Inc

Integration package published:

SPS Commerce Supplier Integration with SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud 

The SPS Commerce Fulfillment Connector for SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud enables businesses to effortlessly automate and optimize electronic data interchange (EDI) with trading partners, streamlining order-to-cash processes and enhancing supply chain visibility. This cloud-based solution offers easy configuration, scalable performance, and a single connection to thousands of retailers, vendors, and logistics providers, reducing manual work and errors while supporting business growth. Integrated directly with SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud and the SAP Business Technology Platform through SAP Integration Suite, it provides real-time monitoring, compliance with trading partner requirements, and 24/7 expert support. By leveraging this connector, companies can consolidate orders across channels, achieve seamless data exchange, and maintain a clean core SAP environment.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

PriceFx AG

Integration packages published:

SAP S/4HANA Pricing Procedure Mapping with Pricefx

This integration package contains sample mappings required to replicate pricing procedures from SAP S/4HANA to Pricefx.

SAP S/4HANA Pricing Procedure Integration with Pricefx

This integration package contains integration flows to replicate pricing procedures from SAP S/4HANA to Pricefx. They can be used as templates to speed up your integration between SAP S/4HANA and Pricefx. You could modify message mappings and value mappings according to your needs.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SirionLabs Pte Ltd

Integration packages published:

Sirion Contract Lifecycle Management Integration with SAP Ariba Supplier Management

The Sirion Contract Lifecycle Management (CLM) Integration with SAP Ariba Supplier Management enables seamless synchronization of supplier between SAP Ariba and Sirion CLM, ensuring a unified and accurate supplier lifecycle across systems.

Supplier requests approved in SAP Ariba triggers the creation of counterparty/supplier in Sirion CLM along with workflow movement ensuring complete synchronization of supplier's information.

Sirion Contract Lifecycle Management Integration with SAP Ariba Contract Workspace

The Sirion Contract Lifecycle Management (CLM) Contract Workspace Adapter for SAP Ariba seamlessly integrates the contracting flow between the two systems. Contract Requests (CDRs) signed in Sirion CLM are automatically transitioned into Contract Workspaces (CWs) in SAP Ariba along with the associated contract documents, enabling synchronization between the two systems throughout the contracting flow.​

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

RealCore Group GmbH

Integration packages published:

SAP LeanIX Integration with SAP API Management

This integration package enables data synchronization between SAP API Management and SAP LeanIX factsheets by extracting relevant API proxies and provider information and transferring them to SAP LeanIX applications.

SAP Cloud ALM and Jira Task Synchronization

This integration package enables comprehensive and bidirectional synchronization of tasks between SAP Cloud ALM and Atlassian Jira, ensuring transparency and alignment across both platforms. It facilitates seamless collaboration between project and development teams by synchronizing all relevant task data in real time.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

KaTe GmbH

Integration package published:

KaTe Confluent Parallel Consumer Adapter

KaTe confluent parallel consumer adapter allows you to scale on record key level or even process records without order if required.

The adapter implements confluent parallel consumer lib to scale on different configurable metrics as single or batch processing option for high volume processing.

The adapter inherits all features of the KaTe Kafka adapter like support for Avro and Protobuf schemas, schema registries as well as sophisticated error handling and monitoring capabilities built into the SAP integrations suite environment.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Thank you very much for your attention, and we look forward to sharing more exciting content in the future.

If you are interested in Partnering with SAP Business Accelerator Hub, please refer to this section for more details.

entry.title: Zertifiziert für die Zukunft: Cpro Industry über SAP BTP, Cloud und Kundenvertrauen
entry.content:
Als SAP Gold Partner und zertifizierter Operations-Partner für SAP S/4HANA und SAP BTP unterstützt die Cpro Industry Projects & Solutions GmbH Unternehmen ganzheitlich – von der Einführung über die Weiterentwicklung bis hin zum Betrieb ihrer SAP-Landschaften. Mit den aktuellen Zertifizierungen unterstreicht das Unternehmen seine ausgewiesene Kompetenz und stärkt zugleich seine Position in zentralen Wachstumsfeldern der SAP-Strategie. Im Interview gibt Cpro Industry Einblicke in die wachsende Rolle von SAP BTP, in ihren Ansatz bei Cloud-Transformationen, in Erfahrungen aus dem Auditprozess sowie in die Bedeutung von Zertifizierungen für die Kundenkommunikation.

Können Sie uns Ihr Unternehmen kurz vorstellen und uns einen kurzen Einblick geben, welche Services Sie Ihren Kunden anbieten?

Die Cpro Industry Projects & Solutions GmbH, kurz Cpro Industry, begleitet Unternehmen bei der Einführung, Weiterentwicklung und dem Betrieb von SAP Lösungen. Als SAP Gold Partner und Teil einer breit aufgestellten Gruppe mit mehreren hundert SAP-Beraterinnen und -Beratern verbinden wir strategische Beratung, Projektumsetzung und langfristigen Application-Management-Service aus einer Hand. Unser Leistungsspektrum reicht von SAP S/4HANA in der Public- und Private-Cloud über SAP BTP und SAP CX bis hin zu branchenspezifischen Themen in Logistik, Supply Chain und Prozessdigitalisierung. Für unsere Kunden ist es vor allem wichtig, dass wir nicht nur die Technologie beherrschen, sondern auch die operativen Abläufe, Anforderungen und Realitäten im Tagesgeschäft verstehen.

2023 haben Sie sich erstmals dazu entschlossen, Ihr Serviceangebot von SAP zertifizieren zu lassen. Was hat Sie damals zu diesem Schritt bewegt, und wie beurteilen Sie diese Entscheidung rückblickend?

Die Entscheidung für die erste Zertifizierung im Jahr 2023 war für uns der logische nächste Schritt. Zum einen wollten wir unseren Status sichern und uns frühzeitig auf die neuen Zertifizierungsmodelle von SAP einstellen. Zum anderen war es uns wichtig, unsere Services weiterzuentwickeln und die Qualität für unsere Kunden noch konsequenter zu schärfen. Eine unabhängige Bestätigung durch SAP hat dabei einen hohen Stellenwert, weil sie unsere Arbeitsweise, Prozesse und fachliche Tiefe auch von außen nachvollziehbar macht. Rückblickend war diese Entscheidung absolut richtig. Die Zertifizierung hat uns nicht nur in unserer Positionierung gestärkt, sondern intern auch dabei geholfen, Standards, Verantwortlichkeiten und Qualitätsansprüche noch klarer zu definieren.

Ende letzten Jahres folgte eine erneute Zertifizierung, diesmal sowohl für SAP S/4HANA operations als auch für SAP BTP operations. Was waren die ausschlaggebenden Gründe, diesen Weg weiterzugehen und Ihr Portfolio erneut zertifizieren zu lassen?

Für uns war dieser Schritt folgerichtig. SAP BTP wird in den kommenden Jahren noch stärker zu einer zentralen Plattform für Erweiterungen, Integration und Innovation in modernen SAP Landschaften. Deshalb war es unser Anspruch, uns auch in diesem Bereich optimal aufzustellen und unsere Kompetenz nachvollziehbar zu belegen. Gleichzeitig begleiten wir seit vielen Jahren Kunden auf dem Weg zu SAP S/4HANA, sowohl in klassischen Transformationsprojekten als auch im Umfeld von RISE und der Private Cloud Edition. Der dazugehörige Betrieb und der anschließende AMS Service sind seit langem ein fester Bestandteil unseres Geschäfts. Vor diesem Hintergrund war es naheliegend, auch diese Leistungen erneut und gezielt zertifizieren zu lassen.

 

Welche Erfahrungen haben Sie in den vergangenen Jahren mit SAP BTP gesammelt, und welchen Einfluss hatten die Zertifizierung und das Audit auf Ihre Weiterentwicklung in diesem Bereich?

Wir haben in den vergangenen Jahren sehr deutlich gesehen, wie stark die Relevanz der SAP BTP zunimmt. In immer mehr Kundenprojekten spielt sie eine wichtige Rolle, etwa bei Integrationsszenarien, bei Erweiterungen nah am SAP Standard oder wenn bestehende SAP Anwendungen zukunftsfähig weiterentwickelt werden sollen. Genau deshalb wird die SAP BTP für uns auch in den nächsten Jahren ein klarer Fokusbereich bleiben. Die Zertifizierung und das Audit hatten für uns nicht nur einen formalen Wert. Sie haben uns geholfen, unsere Strukturen, Methoden und Betriebsprozesse in diesem Bereich weiter zu schärfen. Gleichzeitig unterstützen sie uns dabei, unsere Marktposition als etablierter und verlässlicher Partner für SAP BTP weiter auszubauen.

SAP richtet seine Strategie konsequent auf die Cloud aus und ermutigt Partner, ihre Kunden aktiv bei der Transformation zu begleiten. Wie unterstützen Sie Ihre Kunden derzeit bei ihrer Cloud Migration, und planen Sie, Ihr Leistungsportfolio künftig noch stärker auf Cloud Services auszurichten?

Wir unterstützen unsere Kunden bei der Cloud Transformation immer entlang ihrer konkreten Ausgangslage. Manche Unternehmen kommen aus einem gewachsenen ECC-Umfeld, andere prüfen einen Greenfield-Ansatz, wieder andere gehen mit RISE in die Private Cloud Edition oder bewerten den Weg in die Public Cloud. Unsere Aufgabe ist es, gemeinsam mit dem Kunden ein realistisches Zielbild zu entwickeln, die Transformation fachlich und technisch sauber aufzusetzen und anschließend einen stabilen Betrieb sicherzustellen. Genau darin liegt eine unserer Stärken, weil wir Strategie, Implementierung und Operations miteinander verbinden. Ja, wir werden unser Leistungsportfolio in den kommenden Jahren noch stärker auf Cloud Services ausrichten. Die Cloud ist für SAP und viele unserer Kunden längst kein Randthema mehr, sondern der zentrale Rahmen für künftige Weiterentwicklung, Skalierbarkeit und Innovation.

Welche Rolle spielen die SAP Zertifizierungen für Ihre Marketingaktivitäten und für die Kommunikation mit Ihren bestehenden und potenziellen Kunden?

Die SAP Zertifizierungen spielen für unsere Marktkommunikation eine wichtige Rolle, weil sie Vertrauen schaffen. In einem Umfeld, in dem viele Anbieter mit ähnlichen Leistungsversprechen auftreten, ist eine unabhängige Bestätigung durch SAP ein starkes Signal. Für Bestandskunden sind die Zertifizierungen ein zusätzlicher Beleg dafür, dass sie mit einem Partner zusammenarbeiten, der seine Leistungen strukturiert, nachvollziehbar und auf hohem Qualitätsniveau erbringt. Für potenzielle Kunden sind sie ein relevanter Vertrauensfaktor in der frühen Auswahlphase, in Ausschreibungen sowie in persönlichen Gesprächen. Auch das zugrunde liegende Competency Framework ist für uns wertvoll, weil es sichtbar macht, in welchen Bereichen unsere Kernkompetenzen liegen und wie konsequent wir unsere Services weiterentwickeln.

 

 

Informationen zum Zertifizierungsprogramm für Operations Partner und eine Übersicht über alle zertifizierten Partner finden Sie auf unserem SAP Operations Partner Guide.

Erfahren Sie mehr auf PartnerEdge.com

entry.title: Transforming Manual Sales Order Processing into an Intelligent SAP BTP Workflow
entry.content:
Many organizations still rely on manual, email-driven sales order processing. Business users receive customer documents, extract key details, validate information, and create orders in SAP S/4HANA. While manageable at low volumes, this approach becomes time-consuming, error-prone, and difficult to scale as document complexity and volume increase.

Reimagining the Process with SAP BTP

This solution transforms the traditional process into an intelligent, end-to-end workflow using:

  • SAP Build Process Automation for orchestration
  • SAP Document AI for document understanding
  • SAP CAP (Cloud Application Programming Model) for validation, enrichment, and user interaction

The result is a centralized, application-driven experience that minimizes manual effort while maintaining business control.

Solution Overview

The automated workflow replaces fragmented manual steps with a streamlined process:

  1. Incoming emails are monitored via SAP Build Process Automation
  2. Attachments are sent to SAP Document AI for data extraction
  3. Document AI extracts relevant sales order fields using a custom schema
  4. A CAP application retrieves and processes the extracted data
  5. Additional validation and enrichment is performed (e.g., master data checks, missing fields, amount validation)
  6. Business users review and approve data in a centralized interface before posting to SAP S/4HANA

Architecture Highlights

The solution is built on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP):

  • SAP Build Process Automation → event-driven orchestration
  • SAP Document AI → document extraction
  • SAP CAP → business logic, validation, enrichment, UI
  • SAP S/4HANA → system of record
  • Connectivity & Destinations → secure integration via APIs

This architecture enables scalable automation with human-in-the-loop control.

Why This Approach Matters

Document extraction alone is not enough. True automation requires business context, validation, and enrichment.

The CAP layer bridges this gap by:

  • Combining extracted data with SAP master data
  • Applying business rules and validations
  • Providing a controlled user review interface

This ensures accuracy while reducing manual effort.

Key Advantages

  • Reduced manual handoffs
  • Improved validation and enrichment control
  • Enhanced user experience through a centralized interface
  • Seamless integration with SAP S/4HANA
  • Scalable and future-ready architecture

Business Impact

This solution addresses the core challenge of converting unstructured documents into structured SAP transactions.

Customer results include:

  • ~30% increase in order-processing capacity
  • ~90% reduction in manual effort per transaction
  • Improved accuracy and reduced rework
  • Faster month-end and quarter-end close
  • Cleaner audit trail and compliance

Conclusion

By consolidating extraction, validation, enrichment, and user review into a single workflow, organizations can move from manual processing to intelligent automation - improving efficiency while maintaining control.

If you have any further questions, please leave a comment below or send your inquiries to the email CoE_AI_PlanningSpendExec@global.corp.sap. We will be happy to get back to you.

entry.title: Positioning SAP Integration Suite enterprise-wide
entry.content:
Integration conversations are changing.  Fast.

Your customers are dealing with fragmented landscapes, rising costs, and growing pressure to deliver results across hybrid environments.  The question is no longer “how do we connect systems?”  It’s “how do we simplify and scale without losing control?”

In our upcoming webinar, Positioning SAP Integration Suite enterprise-wide, we’ll walk through what’s happening in the integration market today.  Where competitors shine.  Where gaps exist.  And where you, as a partner, can step in with clarity and confidence.

We’ll also break down how to position SAP Integration Suite as the backbone for enterprise-wide integration.  Think of fewer moving parts, faster innovation cycles, and measurable outcomes your customers can see.

Plus, you’ll get practical guidance.  How to spot the right entry points.  How to shape strong value narratives.  How to build and grow your integration practice over time.

If you’re already familiar with SAP Integration Suite, this session will help you connect the dots and turn strategy into action.

Register now.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2026/mar/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00013512.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #SAPEcosystem #IntegrationStrategy #DigitalTransformation #EnterpriseArchitecture

entry.title: Transforming Healthcare IT Through Integration: SAP’s Comprehensive Ecosystem Approach
entry.content:
As healthcare systems worldwide face mounting pressures to deliver better patient outcomes while managing costs and operational efficiency, the need for intelligent, integrated solutions has never been greater.

SAP’s mission for healthcare is straightforward yet ambitious: help healthcare companies run more efficiently and deliver high-quality, value-based care. This commitment drives our product development and partnership strategies, ensuring our ecosystem solutions address real-world healthcare challenges while enabling organizations to focus on patient care.

From SAP Healthcare Product Management perspective, we are driving four interconnected themes that create our comprehensive ecosystem for modern healthcare transformation. In this blog post, I’ll outline these themes that define our work and provide insights that serve as your foundation for understanding our healthcare innovation approach.

Verticalization of the SAP Business Suite

The SAP Business Suite represents a comprehensive, integrated ecosystem of our cloud-based applications including SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, SAP SuccessFactors, SAP Concur, and other solutions, all built on the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). This integrated approach ensures that applications work together seamlessly along end-to-end business processes, generating trusted business-critical data at its source and leveraging the SAP Business Data Cloud for unified insights and AI-driven capabilities through SAP Business AI and SAP Joule.

For healthcare providers such as hospitals and health systems, verticalization of the SAP Business Suite is crucial to address the unique operational complexities inherent in healthcare delivery. Healthcare organizations require specialized adaptations across multiple areas, such as e.g., regulatory compliance frameworks, quality management systems, workforce management for clinical staff, and analytics that align with healthcare-specific KPIs. Additionally, regional requirements add another layer of complexity- healthcare providers must often comply with country-specific reporting standards for public health institutions or local regulatory frameworks.

The partner ecosystem plays a vital role in delivering this verticalization, as partners bring deep industry expertise, regional knowledge, and specialized solutions that complement SAP’s core platform capabilities. Through collaborative innovation, partners develop industry-specific extensions, address local compliance requirements, and create solutions that bridge the gap between standard business processes and healthcare-specific workflows.

Within the Cloud ERP component of the SAP Business Suite, SAP has developed industry partner opportunity maps that provide clear guidance on priority areas for innovation and collaboration. These maps help partners identify where they can make the greatest impact and seize growth opportunities within specific industries. A dedicated healthcare opportunity map is now available, designed to foster innovation specifically for healthcare providers. This map will continue to evolve and expand based on ongoing feedback from customers and partners to ensure it addresses the most critical needs in the healthcare sector. PartnerEdge partners can access these comprehensive opportunity maps in the PartnerEdge Portal.

We’re also working on verticalizing the SAP BTP and SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC). Central to this collaborative approach is the support of HL7® FHIR® (Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources), the vital healthcare data standard that enables seamless information exchange across systems. SAP Health Data Services for FHIR exemplifies how we’re providing healthcare-specific capabilities that enable partners to build sophisticated solutions more rapidly and reliably.

Patient Administration and Accounting

SAP has strategically evolved its healthcare approach to offer customers enhanced flexibility through multiple transition pathways to next-generation solutions. To support this transformation, SAP provides various options that leverage our partner ecosystem and modern technology stack, allowing organizations to choose the path that best aligns with their specific needs and implementation strategies.

Customers can start their transition by integrating IS-H with Cloud ERP Private (S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition) as an initial step in their digital transformation journey. This approach serves as a bridge solution, allowing organizations to modernize their ERP foundation while maintaining their existing patient management capabilities. The documentation for configuring the IS-H to SAP S/4HANA integration can be found here.

Another or also subsequent pathway leverages SAP partners who offer S/4HANA Add-On solutions specifically designed to serve as successors to SAP Patient Management. Here, SAP has partnered with leading healthcare technology providers to evolve SAP Patient Management (IS-H) capabilities onto a modern technological foundation. These partners have built upon the proven IS-H functionality, migrating it to the advanced S/4HANA platform while enhancing it with innovative features aligned with their specialized product strategies. This approach also supports a structured migration path where custom development can be systematically assessed, refactored, and integrated into the new platform architecture. Partners leverage proven methodologies and tools to analyze existing custom code, identify reusable components, and provide clear transformation strategies that minimize disruption while modernizing the technical foundation. This allows valuable business logic and processes developed over years of IS-H implementation to be preserved and enhanced rather than rebuilt from scratch. In summary, this collaborative approach empowers customers with next-generation solutions that combine SAP’s established healthcare expertise on top of modern technology, partner-driven innovations. These solutions can be found in the SAP Store.

Finally, customers can fully embrace a comprehensive Hospital Information System (HIS) approach, either as a complete single system or through modular solutions that include billing and administrative functionality. This option represents the most transformative pathway, where organizations can leverage advanced HIS solutions, which provide integrated patient administration and accounting capabilities alongside clinical functionality. These systems can serve as complete replacements for traditional IS-H implementations while offering modern integration capabilities with Cloud ERP through standardized APIs and the SAP Business Technology Platform.

All these transition options are designed to ensure investment protection, support clean core principles, and leverage open standards for interoperability, providing customers with flexible pathways based on their specific strategy, boundary conditions, and regional requirements.

Any-EMR

Electronic Medical Records represent the most critical systems in healthcare organizations today, containing comprehensive clinical data and driving essential workflows throughout the patient journey. While the healthcare industry is evolving toward more modular, specialized system architectures and AI-ready data platforms, EMRs currently remain the foundational hub for clinical operations in most healthcare settings. Understanding both this current reality and the emerging trend towards system modularization, SAP’s Any-EMR strategy focuses on enabling seamless, standardized integration with EMR vendors to create modern, future-proof end-to-end experiences.

This approach is designed to eliminate the system dependencies and process bottlenecks that currently plague healthcare operations, while positioning organizations to adapt flexibly as the industry continues its gradual shift toward more modular healthcare technology ecosystems. This strategy extends beyond technical integration - we actively welcome vendors to join the SAP PartnerEdge program, enabling them to leverage our technology stack, collaborate in customer engagement, while contributing to a more connected healthcare ecosystem. Please find the currently available solutions in the SAP Store here as well.

As depicted below, the “integration needs” between healthcare systems and the SAP portfolio span the complete patient journey: registration, treatment documentation, coding, billing and denial management, clinical supply chain operations, and analytics.

 

Our technical approach leverages industry standards, particularly HL7 FHIR, to provide consistent and reliable connections across different EMR systems. SAP Health Data Services for FHIR serves as the foundation, offering a Platform-as-a-Service implementation of the HL7 FHIR standard on SAP BTP that manages health data using healthcare domain models while providing REST and messaging APIs for seamless connectivity.

SAP Integration Suite plays a pivotal role in orchestrating the complete Any-EMR integration ecosystem. Acting as the central integration hub, it connects healthcare provider systems with Cloud ERP and other SAP Business Suite applications through standardized Healthcare APIs. Together with SAP Health Data Services for FHIR the integration Suite manages the complex bi-directional data flows, ensuring data consistency and process alignment across the healthcare and the SAP landscape.

The Integration Suite’s capabilities are enhanced by comprehensive FHIR Integration Packages that are specifically designed for healthcare scenarios. These packages, accessible through the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, include integration flows, implementation guides, and best practices derived from SAP’s own integration experience.

The value proposition extends beyond technical integration to deliver improved user experiences through single sign-on capabilities, unified interfaces, and elimination of duplicate data entry. Real-time integration ensures immediate data synchronization, while pre-built integration packages available through our Business Accelerator Hub significantly reduce implementation timelines and project complexity.

Finally, when applications or modules are connected at a business process level, synergies may emerge that create additional value beyond the individual capabilities of each system. The result is a multiplicative effect where the integrated solution delivers significantly more value than the sum of its parts. This transforms isolated systems into a cohesive ecosystem that reduces operational friction and enhances decision-making.

Innovation Through Ecosystem Collaboration and AI

The future of healthcare innovation lies in a unified data and AI strategy that transforms how organizations harness their most valuable asset - their data. The SAP Business Data Cloud represents a pivotal evolution in this approach, centralizing, harmonizing, and enriching data from both SAP and non-SAP healthcare systems to create a single source of truth that becomes the fuel for advanced AI-driven insights and decision-making. This integrated foundation enables healthcare organizations to enhance patient outcomes, streamline administrative processes, and deploy predictive analytics for better resource management.

Healthcare innovation requires collaboration across the entire ecosystem, which is why SAP partners with organizations throughout the industry through our Business Technology Platform. BTP serves as both an extension platform for customers and a foundational platform for partners, providing comprehensive capabilities spanning application development, automation, data analytics, integration services, artificial intelligence, and platform foundation services.

For customers currently on their digital transformation journey, this creates immediate value - they can connect existing systems like SAP Patient Management to SAP BTP using the HL7 FHIR standard, gradually incorporating intelligent features while protecting their current investments.

Looking ahead, we plan to expand SAP Health Data Services for FHIR to include primary healthcare data products covering administrative and financial data in the coming months. When available, this will make health-related data based on the FHIR domain model natively accessible within SAP Business Data Cloud and, hence, create new opportunities for analytics and AI-powered insights that can transform healthcare delivery and operations. 

Technical Foundation and Clean Core Approach

All transformation pathways emphasize SAP’s clean core philosophy, which ensures that organizations can protect their technology investments while enabling future extensibility and innovation. This approach relies on open standards for interoperability, with SAP Business Technology Platform serving as the integration foundation for all approaches. The clean core methodology supports multiple EMR environments with harmonized integration approaches, ensuring that organizations can adapt and evolve their technology landscapes without being locked into proprietary architectures.

The SAP Integration Suite provides comprehensive connectivity capabilities, while the Business Accelerator Hub offers pre-built integration packages and APIs accessible at api.sap.com. This standardized approach reduces implementation complexity while ensuring that all integrations follow best practices for security, compliance, and performance.

Looking Forward

SAP’s healthcare strategy positions us and our partners to address the evolving needs of healthcare organizations worldwide. By combining our robust technology platform with deep industry expertise and strong partner relationships, we’re creating an ecosystem where innovation thrives and healthcare organizations can focus on what matters most: delivering exceptional patient care.

The future of healthcare is intelligent, integrated, and collaborative. With this approach, we’re not just adapting to this future - we’re actively shaping it through comprehensive solutions that transform how healthcare organizations operate, integrate, and innovate.

 

HL7, FHIR, and the Flame Design mark are registered trademarks of Health Level Seven International.

For deeper insights into our FHIR strategy and integration approaches, explore our related blog posts:

  • Benefits of the HL7 FHIR Industry Standard
  • Integration from IS-H to SAP Health Data Services for FHIR
  • Integrating EMR Systems with SAP S/4HANA Cloud.
  • Healthcare integration - Patient as a business partner in SAP Cloud ERP Private
